channel.title: Microsoft Cloud Platform Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily updates from Azure and Microsoft 365 blogs

item.title: Enabling fallback to internet for Azure Private DNS Zones in hybrid architectures
item.description: Introduction Azure Private Endpoint enables secure connectivity to Azure PaaS services such as: Azure SQL Managed Instance Azure Container Registry Azure Key Vault Azure Storage Account through private IP addresses within a virtual network. When Private Endpoint is enabled for a service, Azure DNS...
item.dc:creator: kirankumar_manchiwar04

item.title: One-click security scanning and org-wide alert triage come to Advanced Security
item.description: We’re shipping two major capabilities that change how security teams enable and act on application security in Azure DevOps: CodeQL default setup makes it possible to enable code scanning across your organization without configuring a single pipeline, and a new combined alerts experience in...
item.dc:creator: Laura Jiang

item.title: Your Oracle Data Is Sitting Next to Microsoft AI. Are You Using It?
item.description: "Your organization runs Oracle on Azure. You've purchased Microsoft Foundry & OpenAI licenses. Why are your AI projects still stuck in proof-of-concept? "Your employees spend hours every week entering or reconciling data from documents, copying information into ERP systems. What would it mean if...
item.dc:creator: ManishChopra

item.title: A demonstration of Virtual Network TAP
item.description: Azure Virtual Network Terminal Access Point (VTAP), at the time of writing in April 2026 in public preview in select regions, copies network traffic from source Virtual Machines to a collector or traffic analytics tool, running as a Network Virtual Appliance (NVA). VTAP creates a full copy of all...
item.dc:creator: Marc de Droog

item.title: Choosing the Right Azure Hosting Model for AI Agents: A Deep Dive into Foundry Hosted Agents
item.description: AI agents are quickly moving from experiments to production‑critical components of modern applications. But while many teams know how to build agents, far fewer are confident they’re hosting them on the right foundation. Most organizations start by deploying agents the same way they deploy...
item.dc:creator: Reenu Saluja

item.title: Supercharge Your Dev Workflows with GitHub Copilot Custom Skills
item.description: The Problem Every team has those repetitive, multi-step workflows that eat up time: Running a sequence of CLI commands, parsing output, and generating a report Querying multiple APIs, correlating data, and summarizing findings Executing test suites, analyzing failures, and producing actionable...
item.dc:creator: sachoudhury

item.title: DevOps Playbook for the Agentic Era
item.description: Practices, Principles, and Strategic Direction Software delivery has entered a new phase. AI agents are no longer confined to autocomplete suggestions in the editor. They are opening pull requests, generating code across multiple files, proposing infrastructure changes, responding to issues with...
item.dc:creator: David Sanchez

item.title: Data Security Posture Reports (Custom Workspace and Charts)
item.description: For more insights on OOB Reports, check out this article. Overview: NOW IN PUBLIC PREVIEW Microsoft Purview Posture Reports provide a clear, outcome‑based view of how effectively data protection ...Update Type: Announcement, Services: Azure Purview, Categories: Security
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Announcing: Minimum billable object size for cooler storage tiers
item.description: For storage accounts with Azure Blob or Data Lake Storage (ADLS) we are introducing a minimum billable object size for the cool, cold and archive access tiers of 128KiB. Objects on those tiers that are smaller than 128 KiB will be billed as 128 KiB objects at the rate of the corresponding tier....
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Public Preview: Azure Site Recovery support for Windows Azure VMs with NVMe disk controllers.
item.description: Azure Site Recovery now supports replication and disaster recovery for Windows Azure Virtual Machines running on NVMe-enabled Generation 2 VM families, such as the Da/Ea/Fa v6-series and Ebsv5/Ebdsv5 in the Azure-to-Azure scenario. This enhancement allows customers to enable disaster recovery for...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Site Recovery support for Windows Azure VMs with NVMe disk controllers.
item.description: Azure Site Recovery now supports replication and disaster recovery for Windows Azure Virtual Machines running on NVMe-enabled Generation 2 VM families, such as the Da/Ea/Fa v6-series and Ebsv5/Ebdsv5 in the Azure-to-Azure scenario. This enhancement allowsUpdate Type: Preview, Services: Virtual...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Building a Scalable IoT Platform for Facility Management with Azure Serverless Services
item.description: The platform follows a microservices architecture with six independently deployable services, all built on Azure Functions v4 with TypeScript. Each service is containerized and deployed to Azure Container Apps. ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │...
item.dc:creator: nishantmv

item.title: Stop juggling package managers—just run `azd update`
item.description: Update the Azure Developer CLI with a single command on any platform and switch release channels. The post Stop juggling package managers—just run `azd update` appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Kristen Womack

item.title: April Patches for Azure DevOps Server
item.description: We are releasing patches for our self‑hosted product, Azure DevOps Server. We strongly recommend that all customers remain on the latest, most secure version to ensure optimal protection and reliability. The latest release of Azure DevOps Server is available from the download page. This patch...
item.dc:creator: Gloridel Morales

item.title: Azure Arc Server Mar 2026 Forum Recap
item.description: Please find the recording for the monthly Azure Arc Server Forum on YouTube! During the March 2026 Azure Arc Server Forum, we discussed: Deploying Ansible Playbooks through Machine Configuration as Azure Policy (Learn more: Announcing Private Preview: Deploy Ansible Playbooks using Azure Policy via...
item.dc:creator: Aurnov_Chattopadhyay

item.title: Data Security Posture Reports
item.description: Proving Your Data Security Posture with Confidence Microsoft Purview Posture Reports help organizations prove (not just assume) that their data security controls are working. They provide a clear, ...Update Type: Announcement, Services: Azure Purview, Categories: Security
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Demystifying On-Demand Capacity Reservations
item.description: About On-Demand Capacity Reservations Introducing the “parking garage” metaphor There are dozens of VM types available in Azure which span multiple generations of CPU across vendors and architectures. Within each Azure region are datacenters hosting pools of hardware which runs Azure services, such...
item.dc:creator: KenHooverMSFT

item.title: Generally Available: Smart Tier (Azure Blob and Data Lake Storage)
item.description: Announcing the GA of smart tier for Azure Blob and Data Lake Storage (ADLS). Smart Tier is Generally Available (GA) today in nearly all zonal public cloud regions. (The Azure regions Israel Central, Qatar Central and UAE North are not part of this GA announcement) Smart tier is a fully managed and...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Smart tier (Azure Blob and Data Lake Storage)
item.description: Announcing the GA of smart tier for Azure Blob and Data Lake Storage (ADLS). Smart Tier is Generally Available (GA) today in nearly all zonal public cloud regions. (The Azure regions Israel Central, Qatar Central and UAE North are not part of this GA annoUpdate Type: GA, Services: Data Lake...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Running KQL queries on Microsoft Sentinel data lake using API
item.description: Co-Authors: Zeinab Mokhtarian Koorabbasloo and Matthew Lowe As security data lakes become the backbone of modern analytics platforms, organizations need new ways to operationalize their data. While interactive tools and portals support data exploration, many real-world workflows increasingly...
item.dc:creator: Zeinab Mokhtarian Koorabbasloo

item.title: Qurious About Quantum?
item.description: Why IT Should Care About Quantum Right Now – It’s Not Just A Research Problem Today on April 14th, 2026 we celebrate World Quantum Day! There has been a noticeable rise in the topic of quantum computing appearing across technical conferences, business news, and strategic planning conversations. But...
item.dc:creator: JohnGruszczyk

item.title: Take your PostgreSQL-backed apps to the next level
item.description: PostgreSQL is a powerful and hugely popular database engine, and it really comes alive across Microsoft developer platforms. You can build with PostgreSQL across Azure offerings, develop productively in Visual Studio Code with strong extensions and tooling, and connect your data to agentic...
item.dc:creator: Ismael Mejía Useche

item.title: New Aspire database integrations
item.description: Aspire 13.2 brings MongoDB Entity Framework Core and Azure Data Lake Storage integrations, plus quality-of-life improvements to existing database clients. The post New Aspire database integrations appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: Alistair Matthews

item.title: Build Multi-Agent AI Apps on Azure App Service with Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0
item.description: Part 1 of 3 — Multi-Agent AI on Azure App Service This is part 1 of a 3 part series on deploying and working with multi-agent AI on Azure App Service. Follow allong to learn how to deploy, manage, observe, and secure your agents on Azure App Service. A couple of months ago, we published a...
item.dc:creator: jordanselig

item.title: Monitor AI Agents on App Service with OpenTelemetry and the New Application Insights Agents View
item.description: Part 2 of 3: In Blog 1, we deployed a multi-agent travel planner on Azure App Service using the Microsoft Agent Framework (MAF) 1.0 GA. This post dives deep into how we instrumented those agents with OpenTelemetry and lit up the brand-new Agents (Preview) view in Application Insights. 📋...
item.dc:creator: jordanselig

item.title: Microsoft Sentinel data federation: Expand visibility while preserving governance
item.description: Security data volumes are growing faster than ever, but visibility across the entire digital estate hasn’t kept pace. As organizations expand across cloud, hybrid, and SaaS environments, critical security-relevant data is increasingly stored across multiple data stores due to governance and...
item.dc:creator: chaitra_satish

item.title: Capacity Scheduler: Smarter capacity control for Eventhouse
item.description: Modern, real-time analytics workloads are rarely flat. In Eventhouse, some of the customers consistently told us that their usage follows clear, predictable patterns: heavy ingestion during business hours, lighter query traffic overnight, quiet weekends, and short but critical pipeline windows....
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Unifying “Analyze data with” analytics across Fabric
item.description: As Microsoft Fabric continues to converge analytics experiences across workloads, one of the most important steps forward is reducing friction in how users move from raw data to insights. With the latest integrations, the Eventhouse Endpoint is now deeply embedded into the “Analyze data with” entry...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: From CI/CD to Continuous AI: The Future of GitHub Automation
item.description: Introduction For over a decade, CI/CD (Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment) has been the backbone of modern software engineering. It helped teams move from manual, error-prone deployments to automated, reliable pipelines. But today, we are standing at the edge of another...
item.dc:creator: Ravindra_Kumar_Vishwakarma

item.title: Optimize object storage costs automatically with smart tier—now generally available
item.description: Optimize object storage costs automatically with smart tier—now generally available By continuously optimizing data placement, smart tier ensures your storage costs are aligned with actual usage.Update Type: Announcement, Services: Data Lake Storage, Azure Storage, Categories:
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Generally Available: Azure File Sync in Belgium Central, Malaysia West, and Indonesia Central
item.description: Azure File Sync enables seamless tiering of data from on-premises Windows Servers to Azure Files for hybrid use cases and simplified migration. It also enables you to leverage the performance, flexibility and compatibility of your on-premises File Server while leveraging the scale and cost...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure File Sync in Belgium Central, Malaysia West, and Indonesia Central
item.description: Azure File Sync enables seamless tiering of data from on-premises Windows Servers to Azure Files for hybrid use cases and simplified migration. It also enables you to leverage the performance, flexibility and compatibility of your on-premises File ServerUpdate Type: GA, Services: Azure Storage,...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Microsoft Purview Referential Architecture Diagrams
item.description: Microsoft Purview architecture diagrams provide a reference view of how classification, sensitivity labelling, Data Loss Prevention (DLP), Insider Risk Management, and Microsoft 365 Copilot protectio...Update Type: Announcement, Services: Azure Purview, Categories: Management
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Govern AI Agents on App Service with the Microsoft Agent Governance Toolkit
item.description: Part 3 of 3 — Multi-Agent AI on Azure App Service In Blog 1, we built a multi-agent travel planner with Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 on App Service. In Blog 2, we added observability with OpenTelemetry and the new Application Insights Agents view. Now in Part 3, we secure those agents for...
item.dc:creator: jordanselig

item.title: Public Preview: Managed identity support for graphical session recording
item.description: Today, we are announcing Azure Bastion now supports managed identities for graphical session recording. Azure Bastion graphical session recording now supports write access to storage accounts using managed identities. With this update, users have the option to configure managed identities during...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Managed identity support for graphical session recording
item.description: Today, we are announcing Azure Bastion now supports managed identities for graphical session recording. Azure Bastion graphical session recording now supports write access to storage accounts using managed identities. With this update, users have the optiUpdate Type: Preview, Services: Virtual...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Generally Available: Azure Storage Mover now available in Azure Government (US)
item.description: Azure Storage Mover is now available in Azure Government (US), enabling U.S. government customers and partners to securely migrate large‑scale file data to Azure Government cloud environments using a fully managed migration service. What is Azure Storage Mover? Azure Storage Mover is a fully...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Storage Mover now available in Azure Government (US)
item.description: Azure Storage Mover is now available in Azure Government (US), enabling U.S. government customers and partners to securely migrate large‑scale file data to Azure Government cloud environments using a fully managed migration service. What is Azure StorageUpdate Type: GA, Services: Azure Storage,...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Generally Available: Granular Encryption-in-Transit Controls for SMB and NFS on Azure Files
item.description: Azure Files now supports independent configuration of encryption-in-transit settings for SMB and NFS protocols at the storage account level. Customers can now define protocol-specific security policies, enabling precise control over encryption requirements for each protocol without compromise. This...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Public Preview: Monitor AKS applications with OpenTelemetry and Azure Monitor
item.description: Azure Monitor now supports monitoring applications that run on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) by using OpenTelemetry for instrumentation and data collection.Leverage autoinstrumentation to deploy the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry distro to workloads or use autoconfiguration to route OTLP signals from...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Monitor AKS applications with OpenTelemetry and Azure Monitor
item.description: Azure Monitor now supports monitoring applications that run on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) by using OpenTelemetry for instrumentation and data collection.Leverage autoinstrumentation to deploy the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry distro to workloads or useUpdate Type: Preview, Services: Kubernetes...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Granular Encryption-in-Transit Controls for SMB and NFS on Azure Files
item.description: Azure Files now supports independent configuration of encryption-in-transit settings for SMB and NFS protocols at the storage account level. Customers can now define protocol-specific security policies, enabling precise control over encryption requirementUpdate Type: GA, Services: Azure Storage,...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Cascading Read Replicas Now Generally Available!
item.description: We’re excited to announce the General Availability of cascading read replicas in Azure Database for PostgreSQL. This capability allows you to create read replicas for your Azure Database for PostgreS...Update Type: GA, Services: Database for PostgreSQL, Categories:
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: Azure Managed Grafana Essential SKU will retire on March 30, 2027
item.description: Azure Managed Grafana Essential SKU will be retired on March 30, 2027.Customers using the Essential SKU are encouraged to transition to Azure Managed Grafana Standard SKU or Azure Monitor Dashboards with Grafana before the retirement date to avoid service disruption, including loss of access to...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Managed Grafana Essential SKU will retire on March 30, 2027
item.description: Azure Managed Grafana Essential SKU will be retired on March 30, 2027.Customers using the Essential SKU are encouraged to transition to Azure Managed Grafana Standard SKU or Azure Monitor Dashboards with Grafana before the retirement date to avoid service [Sevice: Managed Grafana, Target Date: 01...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Public Preview: StandardV2 NAT Gateway as an outbound type for AKS
item.description: AKS now supports managed and user-assigned StandardV2 NAT Gateway as an outbound type for your AKS managed or BYO VNets. This unlocks zone-redundant outbound connectivity, up to 100 Gbps outbound throughput, and IPv6 outbound support for AKS clusters.Learn more.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: StandardV2 NAT Gateway as an outbound type for AKS
item.description: AKS now supports managed and user-assigned StandardV2 NAT Gateway as an outbound type for your AKS managed or BYO VNets. This unlocks zone-redundant outbound connectivity, up to 100 Gbps outbound throughput, and IPv6 outbound support for AKS clusters.LearUpdate Type: Preview, Services: Kubernetes...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: GA: DCasv6 and ECasv6 confidential VMs based on 4th Generation AMD EPYC™ processors
item.description: Today, Azure has expanded its confidential computing offerings with the general availability of the DCasv6 and ECasv6 confidential VMs. Regional availability April 13 2026: West Europe Jan 30 2026: Canada Central, Canada East, Norway East, Norway West, Italy North, Germany North, France South,...
item.dc:creator: Rakeshginjupalli

item.title: Connecting an ExpressRoute circuit to Megaport Virtual Edge
item.description: Megaport is an ExpressRoute partner in many locations. The Megaport Cloud Router (MCR) allows ExpressRoute customers to connect leased lines to their on-premise locations, and to connect other Cloud Providers. MCR is easy to set up and operate, it even automatically configures the ExpressRoute...
item.dc:creator: Marc de Droog

item.title: One platform, many insights: How Eventhouse brings analytics together
item.description: Modern analytics isn’t just about storing data. It’s about detecting issues early, understanding them fast, and acting with confidence. Eventhouse in Microsoft Fabric brings advanced analytics capabilities together so teams can move from raw events to insight and action without stitching tools or...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft
